projectTitle: AI-Based Rockfall Prediction and Alert System for Open-Pit Mines
description: A comprehensive web application for real-time monitoring, prediction, and alerting of rockfall events in open-pit mines using AI, integrated with a helpful chatbot for user assistance.

pages.name: Dashboard
pages.description: A real-time overview of the mine's status, featuring key alerts, sensor data, and risk summaries.

components.title: Current Risk Overview
components.description: A static image of the main pit with current risk-level overlays.

pages.name: Live Risk Map
pages.description: An interactive 3D map visualizing the Digital Elevation Model (DEM) data with real-time, AI-predicted rockfall risk overlays.

pages.name: Alert History
pages.description: A searchable and filterable log of all past rockfall alerts.

pages.name: Reports
pages.description: Generate and download historical reports on system performance and mine safety.

chatbotConfig.persona: Your friendly and knowledgeable mine safety assistant, 'Geo'. ⛏️
chatbotConfig.greeting: Welcome! I'm Geo. How can I help you navigate the rockfall system today? You can ask me to 'show the live map' or 'what does DEM mean?'.
chatbotConfig.idlePrompt: Is there anything else I can help you with?
capabilities: Explain technical terms (e.g., DEM, Infrared Sensor).
Answer basic questions about system features.
Execute simple commands like generating a report.

userPrompts: Show me the live risk map
I want to see the 3D model
Where can I see the pit visualization?
I need to see the alert log

information.response: A **Digital Elevation Model (DEM)** is a 3D digital representation of a terrain's surface. We use it to map the open-pit mine's structure and analyze slopes for potential rockfall.

userPrompts: What do the colors on the map mean?
What is the difference between high and critical risk?
information.response: The risk levels are color-coded for quick identification: **Green** is Low Risk, **Yellow** is Moderate, **Orange** is High, and **Red** is Critical. A Critical risk indicates a very high probability of an imminent rockfall.

userPrompts: What does the infrared sensor measure?
information.response: The infrared sensor measures the distance to the rock face. By detecting tiny changes in this distance over time, the AI can identify ground movement that might indicate an unstable area before a rockfall occurs.

actions.response: I've started generating the Daily Summary report for you. It will be available for download in the Reports section shortly.

userPrompts: Mark the critical alert as seen
actions.response: Acknowledged. The latest critical alert has been marked as seen.

userPrompts: Why is the sensor feed empty?
I don't see any sensor data
troubleshooting.response: If the sensor feed is blank, it could be a connection issue or the sensor might be undergoing maintenance. Please check the system status page or contact a system administrator if the problem persists.
